---
title: "Try it"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite: Try it"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
S = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/site_stats.json"))
```

::: {.eyebrow}
Run it locally · sign in · talk to Sara
:::

Two paths. The offline one proves the machinery with no keys and no services. The full one gets you a real conversation with the assistant.

## Prove it offline, no keys

Needs [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) (it manages Python 3.12).

```bash
make setup     # venv + locked dependencies
make check     # lint, the full test suite, leak check, eval gate
```

`make check` runs the full `{python} S["tests"]`-test suite, the domain-leak linter, and the CI eval gate, all on deterministic fakes. Green means the pipeline, the gates, and the evaluation harness all work. `make doctor` explains anything missing.

## Run the real thing

Needs Docker and two keys (Groq and Cohere, both have free tiers).

```bash
cp .env.example .env       # add GROQ_API_KEY and COHERE_API_KEY
make up                    # Qdrant, Postgres, Neo4j, MLflow
make dbt-build             # build + test the semantic layer
make ingest                # build the vector index
make graph-load            # load the knowledge graph
make serve                 # API on :8000
cd web && npm install && npm run dev    # storefront on :3000
```

Open the storefront, sign in as the demo shopper (`demo` / `Canada54321`), and open the chat. You are now Aaron Esteki, a member with a purchase history and orders in transit, which is what makes the order and account questions real.

## What to say to Sara

::: {.grid-cards}
::: {.card}
### Ask about your orders
"Where's my order?" or "which of my packages are on the way?" Your signed-in identity unlocks your own orders, so there is no verification quiz.
:::
::: {.card}
### Ask by need, not keyword
"A gift for my mum who loves yoga, around \$60" or "a warm waterproof jacket for a rainy winter." It reasons about recipient, use, weather, and budget.
:::
::: {.card}
### Push on the details
"Does the Flow Legging run small?" pulls a real critical review. "What's your return policy?" answers from policy text, cited.
:::
::: {.card}
### Complain, or ask for a person
"My order is late and I'm annoyed" gets empathy and a next step. "Get me a human" reaches Tiffany, who files a ready case.
:::
::: {.card}
### Try to break privacy
Sign out and ask for orders with only an email, or ask "who bought the Flow Legging?". Both are refused in code, before the model runs.
:::
::: {.card}
### Talk instead of typing
Voice mode speaks one or two sentences and puts the fuller set on screen. Interrupt it mid-reply; it stops on your first words.
:::
:::

## The honest edges

- A determined phrasing against a guide listing both a men's and a women's option can still slip one cross-gender item through. The common cases are handled; this rare one is [documented](reliability.qmd).
- The store-to-product graph relationship is thin (see [Decisions](decisions.qmd)), so "which stores carry X" returns nothing for most of the catalog.
- It is a synthetic single-store demo. The engineering is the point; the scale is deliberately small.

## Useful extras

| Command | What it does |
| --- | --- |
| `make reproduce` | The whole offline verification from a clean clone, one command |
| `make eval` | Retrieval and abstain quality against the `{python} S["golden_items"]`-item golden set |
| `make drift` | Drift across the five monitors from recent traffic |
| `make shadow` | Champion vs challenger replay on real traffic, before a human promotes |
| `make site-stats` | Regenerates the numbers this site reads |

---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Built by **Aaron (Hooman) Esteki**. A working agentic RAG platform with a knowledge graph, a governed data layer, and deterministic safety guards. Full source, including this site, is on [GitHub](https://github.com/hoomanesteki/agentic-rag-knowledge-ai-platform). More at [esteki.ca](https://esteki.ca).
:::
